title: erinus viscosus
intro: Erinus viscosus, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky cranesbill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Montiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. Erinus viscosus is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and has been used to treat conditions like diarrhea and dysentery. The herbal preparations of Erinus viscosus include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
